##NAMES=Magnesiofluckite
##RRUFFID=R250052
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=CaMg(AsO_3_OH)_2_(H_2_O)_2_
##LOCALITY=Torrecillas mine, Iquique Province, Chile
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Colourless tablets and short blades often grouped in tightly intergrown aggregates. Associated with  camanchacaite (R250031), chinchorroite (R250032),  espadaite (R250051),  picaite (R250053) and ríosecoite (R250054). Type sample described in Kampf et al (2019)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250052
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_0.99_Na_0.01_)Mg_1.00_[AsO_3_OH]_2_(H_2_O)_2_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.4143 b: 7.5321 c: 6.8917 alpha: 82.477 beta: 97.682 gamma: 95.379 volume: 427.84 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
